channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
item.description: As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New stealthy Quasar Linux malware targets software developers
item.description: A previously undocumented Linux implant named Quasar Linux (QLNX) is targeting developers' systems with a mix of rootkit, backdoor, and credential-stealing capabilities. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Instructure hacker claims data theft from 8,800 schools, universities
item.description: The hacker behind a breach at education technology giant Instructure claims to have stolen 280 million data records for students and staff from 8,809 colleges, school districts, and online education p...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trellix Source Code Breach Highlights Growing Supply Chain Threats
item.description: Info is scant, but such breaches can reveal where a security product's controls are located and how detections are designed, giving attackers a leg up....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: DAEMON Tools trojanized in supply-chain attack to deploy backdoor
item.description: Hackers trojanized installers for the DAEMON Tools software and since April 8, delivered a backdoor to thousands of systems that downloaded the product from the official website. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Student hacked Taiwan high-speed rail to trigger emergency brakes
item.description: A 23-year-old university student in Taiwan was arrested for interfering with the TETRA communication system used by the country's high-speed railway network (THSR). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Apache HTTP/2 Flaw (CVE-2026-23918) Enables DoS and Potential RCE
item.description: The Apache Software Foundation (ASF) has released security updates to address several security vulnerabilities in the HTTP Server, including a severe vulnerability that could potentially lead to remot...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: DAEMON Tools Supply Chain Attack Compromises Official Installers with Malware
item.description: A newly identified supply chain attack targeting DAEMON Tools software has compromised its installers to serve a malicious payload, according to findings from Kaspersky. "These installers are distribu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
item.description: A proof-of-concept exploit (PoC) shows how someone with admin privileges can exploit the issue to steal passwords, and thus use them to engage in further malicious activity....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FTC to ban data broker Kochava from selling Americans’ location data
item.description: The FTC will ban data broker Kochava and its subsidiary, Collective Data Solutions (CDS), from selling location data without consumers' explicit consent to settle charges alleging that it sold precise...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked UAT-8302 Targets Governments Using Shared APT Malware Across Regions
item.description: A sophisticated China-nexus advanced persistent threat (APT) group has been attributed to attacks targeting government entities in South America since at least late 2024 and government agencies in sou...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The EOL Blind Spot in Your CVE Feed: What SCA Tools Miss
item.description: Critical vulnerabilities can exist in open source software your scanners don't check. HeroDevs reveals how EOL software creates blind spots in CVE feeds and SCA tools, and how you can receive a free e...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Vimeo data breach exposes personal information of 119,000 people
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information belonging to over 119,000 people after hacking the Vimeo online video platform in April, according to data breach notification service Have I...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
item.description: Every AI tool, workflow automation, and productivity app your employees connected to Google or Microsoft this year left something behind: a persistent OAuth token with no expiration date, no automatic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How the Story of a USB Penetration Test Went Viral
item.description: Two decades ago, pen tester Steve Stasiukonis caused a sensation by sprinkling rigged thumb drives around a credit union parking lot and following what curious employees did next. This episode looks b...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: MetInfo CMS CVE-2026-29014 Exploited for Remote Code Execution Attacks
item.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw impacting an open-source content management system (CMS) known as MetInfo, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google now offers up to $1.5 million for some Android exploits
item.description: Google overhauls its Android and Chrome vulnerability rewards programs, offering bounties of up to $1.5 million for the most difficult exploits while scaling back payouts for flaws that artificial int...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: We Scanned 1 Million Exposed AI Services. Here's How Bad the Security Actually Is
item.description: While the software industry has made genuine strides over the past few decades to deliver products securely, the furious pace of AI adoption is putting that progress at risk. Businesses are moving fas...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Karakurt extortion gang ‘cold case’ negotiator gets 8.5 years in prison
item.description: A Latvian national extradited to the United States was sentenced to 8.5 years in prison for his "cold case" negotiator role in the Russian Karakurt ransomware group. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CloudZ malware abuses Microsoft Phone Link to steal SMS and OTPs
item.description: A new version of the CloudZ remote access tool (RAT) is deploying a previously unseen malicious plugin called Pheno that hijacks the Microsoft Phone Link connection to steal sensitive codes from mobil...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ScarCruft Hacks Gaming Platform to Deploy BirdCall Malware on Android and Windows
item.description: The North Korea-aligned state-sponsored hacking group known as ScarCruft has compromised a video game platform in a supply chain espionage attack, trojanizing its components with a backdoor called Bir...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ScarCruft hackers push BirdCall Android malware via game platform
item.description: The North Korean hacker group APT37 has been delivering an Android version of a backdoor called BirdCall in a supply-chain attack through a video game platform. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Weaver E-cology RCE Flaw CVE-2026-22679 Actively Exploited via Debug API
item.description: A critical security vulnerability in Weaver (Fanwei) E-cology, an enterprise office automation (OA) and collaboration platform, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability (CVE-2...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Details Phishing Campaign Targeting 35,000 Users Across 26 Countries
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a large-scale credential theft campaign that has leveraged a combination of code of conduct-themed lures and legitimate email services to direct users to attacker-co...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Weaver E-cology critical bug exploited in attacks since March
item.description: Hackers have been exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-22679) in the Weaver E-cology office automation since mid-March to run discovery commands. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Physical Cargo Theft Gets a Boost From Cybercriminals
item.description: Cargo theft is no longer about small groups of criminals operating on the ground, but transnational cybercriminal syndicates using access to supply chain systems to reroute goods....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: RMM Tools Fuel Stealthy Phishing Campaign
item.description: Attackers are abusing two remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools to evade detection in a campaign that has impacted over 80 organizations so far....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Researchers report Amazon SES abused in phishing to evade detection
item.description: Cybersecurity firm Kaspersky reports that the Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) is being increasingly abused to send convincing phishing emails that can bypass standard security filters and render rep...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
item.description: Shortly after the authentication-bypass flaw was disclosed multiple proof-of-concept exploits appeared, and one researcher claims there's been zero-day activity for at least a month....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Phishing Campaign Hits 80+ Orgs Using SimpleHelp and ScreenConnect RMM Tools
item.description: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025 with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
